qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: GraphQL API denial-of-service via default unbounded query depth, complexity, batch size, and result limits
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory root cause is unauthenticated activation-email resend in actionSendActivationEmail() accepting arbitrary userId without ownership verification. This finding concerns GraphQL query limit defaults and falsy limit checks in GraphqlController/Gql, so it does not identify the advisory-described vulnerable endpoint, guard, or source-to-sink path.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies Craft CMS UsersController::actionSendActivationEmail(), the send-activation-email action, the arbitrary userId body parameter, or the missing ownership/permission check for pending-user activation email resends described by GHSA-234q-vvw3-mrfq.
evidence: GHSA-234q-vvw3-mrfq describes an unauthenticated activation-email resend endpoint that accepts arbitrary userId values and lacks ownership verification for pending users.
The only final qualifying finding in the judge input manifest is graphql_dos_unbounded_limits.

graphql_dos_unbounded_limits reports unbounded GraphQL depth, complexity, batch size, and result limits, with code locations in GeneralConfig.php, GraphqlController.php, Gql.php, and ElementResolver.php.

The GraphQL DoS finding does not mention actionSendActivationEmail(), send-activation-email, activation emails, userId ownership, pending-user activation, or the vulnerable user-controller path from the advisory.
blockers_to_higher_tier: No advisory-matched qualifying finding exists, so Tier1, Tier2, and Tier3 do not apply.
